mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW M2 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW M2 engine typically costs £4,500–£10,000+ supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — from the N55 in the standard M2 at £4,500–£7,000 to the S55/S58 at £7,000–£10,000+. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M2 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S58B30 in the G87 — widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern performance engines. The S55B30 in the F87 Competition/CS is proven but carries the crank hub caveat. The N55B30T0 in the standard F87 is robust but the crank hub is still a concern on modified cars.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M2 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but a modified S55 without a crank hub fix is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The crank hub is the Achilles heel of the S55. A G87 with S58 has no such caveat. A standard F87 N55 is the safest entry point — but budget for crank hub prevention if you plan to modify it.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my M2 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: By engine: sudden loss of power and metallic noise (crank hub spin on S55/S58), rattling on cold start (N55/S55 timing chain tensioner), blue smoke on startup (N55/S55 valve stem seals). On the S55, any unusual engine noise at high RPM is a warning sign. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £4,500–£10,000+ engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW M2 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M2's values are strong enough to justify engine work. Against a £35,000 F87 Competition, a £7,000–£10,000+ S55 recon is 20–29% — ✅ safe. Against a £60,000 G87, a £7,000–£10,000+ S58 recon is 12–17% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW M2 should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: F87 M2 Competition with the S55 — 405 bhp, proven engine, and depreciated into sensible territory (£30,000–£38,000). Budget for a crank hub fix (£1,500–£3,000) or buy one that's already been done. For the purist, the F87 standard with the N55 is the most playful M2 — but it's less powerful and the interior is dated. For the future, the G87 with the S58 is the most reliable M2 ever made — and the last manual M car.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW M2 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation and variant (F87 Standard, F87 Competition, F87 CS, G87) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: BMW M2 vs M3 — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mechanically they share engines — the S55 in the F87 Competition/CS is the same engine as the F80 M3; the S58 in the G87 is the same engine as the G80 M3. The M2 is lighter and works the powertrain less hard; the M3 is heavier but benefits from the same engineering. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The M2 offers more engagement; the M3 offers more practicality.

hero.tagPill: BMW M2 • 2016–2021, 2023–present • 2 Generations (F87, G87) • N55, S55, S58
hero.h1: M2 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW M2 engine across two generations — from the N55 straight-six of the original F87 to the S55 in the Competition and CS, and the S58 in the G87. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's most engaging modern M car. Backed by real UK M2 engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The M2 rule — pay to play: an S55/S58 recon at £7,000–£10,000+ against a £35,000–£60,000 car is 12–29% — ✅ safe on most examples. On a high-mileage F87 N55 at £25,000, a £4,500–£7,000 recon is 18–28% — still safe. The M2's values are strong enough to justify engine work.
★★★★☆ — the N55 is robust, the S55 is proven, and the S58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern performance engines
S58B30 (G87) — the latest M engine is proving exceptionally robust
N55B30T0 (F87 standard) — crank hub spin risk on modified cars • S55B30 (F87 Competition/CS) — crank hub and timing chain issues
F87 M2 Competition with S55 — 405 bhp, proven engine, depreciated, and the sweet spot in the range
G87 M2 with S58 — 453 bhp (manual) / 480 bhp (auto), the most reliable M engine, and the last manual M car
🏆 The Driver's M Car — the most engaging M model and the last with a manual gearbox

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The M2 is the purest modern M car — compact, rear-wheel-drive, and available with a manual gearbox. The engines are robust, the values are strong, and the driving experience is unmatched. Buy the newest S58 you can afford, or hunt for a well-maintained S55 Competition — and on any modified car, ask about the crank hub.

rows.value: N55/S55 — crank hub spin (especially modified cars)
H2: BMW M2 Generations — Find Your Vehicle

rows.metric: Sub-headline: Two generations, three powertrains, and one mission — to be the most engaging driver's car BMW makes. The right M2 depends on your budget and which engine you want.

rows.value: Our Verdict: The original M2 — the car that recaptured the spirit of the E46 M3. The N55 is robust, the chassis is magnificent, and the values are still sensible. The crank hub is the only caveat — especially on modified cars. The purest M2 experience.

rows.metric: Our Verdict: The M2 with the M3/M4 engine — 405 bhp, improved suspension and brakes, and a more serious character. The S55 is proven and powerful, but the crank hub is the Achilles heel. The sweet spot in the used M2 range — more power than the standard car, less money than the CS.

rows.value: Our Verdict: The ultimate F87 — 444 bhp, carbon fibre everywhere, and produced in just 2,200 examples. A future classic and a collector's item. The S55 is proven, but the crank hub is still the caveat. Buy it as an investment, drive it as a driver's car.

Our Verdict: The second-generation M2 — bigger, more powerful, and built on the CLAR platform shared with the G80 M3. The S58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern performance engines. Available with a six-speed manual — the last manual M car. The G87 is the M2 for the modern era.
The Engine Choice — The Line That Matters Most

rows.metric: The one to buy if budget allows
rows.value: Read the full comparison: BMW M2 F87 vs G87 →

rows.metric: The Crank Hub Question — The Achilles Heel of the S55
rows.value: Correction applied (internal): The S55 engine in the M2 Competition and CS shares the crank hub weakness with the M3/M4. The crank hub is not keyed to the crankshaft — it relies on friction alone. Under high load (especially on tuned cars), the hub can spin, causing catastrophic piston-to-valve contact. This is the defining risk of the S55-powered M2s. The S58 in the G87 has a revised design that addresses this weakness.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW M2 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW M2 — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: N55, S55 and S58 ratings: 90% confidence — these engines are well-documented across the BMW M range with clear patterns from specialist rebuilders and enthusiast communities. N55 data is drawn from the wider N55 family (830+ JLR-wide enquiries). S55 and S58 data is pattern-data-tier due to lower volume but consistent with M3/M4 patterns. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The M2's N55 is the N55B30T0 — a unique variant of the N55 developed specifically for the M2. It is not the same as the N55 found in the 3 Series or 5 Series. The engine uses different pistons, connecting rods, crankshaft bearings, and a unique cooling system. Correction applied: The S55 in the M2 Competition is detuned from the M3/M4 application but shares the same fundamental architecture. The crank hub weakness applies equally to both. Correction applied: The S58 in the G87 produces 453 bhp with the manual transmission and 480 bhp with the automatic. This is a detuned version of the M3/M4 S58 but shares the same robust architecture.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the M2 range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The defining S55 issue — the crank hub is not keyed to the crankshaft and relies on friction alone. Under high load (especially on tuned cars), it can spin, causing piston-to-valve contact. Affects F87 Competition and CS. £7,000–£10,000+ recon.
The N55 shares the same crank hub design weakness as the S55. On modified cars with increased power, the hub can spin. Affects F87 standard M2 with aftermarket tuning.
The S55's timing chain tensioner can wear, causing rattling on cold start. Skipping oil changes or using the wrong oil accelerates wear. Affects F87 Competition/CS.
The S58's known weak point — coolant expansion tank leaks and water pump failures. Affects G87 M2 (2023+). £400–£800 repair, not catastrophic if caught early.
The N55's common issue — oil filter housing gasket leaks causing oil loss. Affects F87 standard M2. £500–£1,200 repair.
Not an engine failure — but a common non-engine M2 enquiry. The F87's rear subframe and differential mounts can fatigue under hard use. Check on any tracked or high-mileage F87.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW M2 Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — the F87 is a modern classic [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — new generation entering used market [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: The M2 generates a different kind of enquiry than the rest of the BMW range — owners are enthusiasts doing due diligence on specific known issues (crank hub, timing chain) rather than families facing unexpected failures. The M2 buyer knows what they're getting into — they just want to know which engine and which year to choose. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The F87 M2 Competition is the sweet spot in the used market — 405 bhp, the S55 engine, and the M3/M4's proven powertrain in a smaller, more engaging package. The crank hub is the only caveat — budget for a hub fix (£1,500–£3,000) or buy one that's already been done. (Editorial synthesis.)

The G87 M2 with the S58 is the future — 453–480 bhp, the most reliable modern M engine, and the last manual M car. Too new for volume reliability data, but the S58 is proving exceptionally robust in the M3/M4. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW M2 Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one M2 based on this page, buy an F87 M2 Competition with the S55 — it's the sweet spot in the range: 405 bhp, proven engine, and depreciated into sensible territory. Just budget for a crank hub fix (£1,500–£3,000) or buy one that's already been done. If your budget stretches, the G87 with the S58 is the most reliable M2 ever made — and the last manual M car.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW M2 Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW M2 engine typically takes 12–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The M2 rule — pay to play: A £7,000–£10,000+ S55/S58 recon against a £35,000–£60,000 car sits at 12–29% — ✅ safe on most examples. Against a £25,000 high-mileage F87 N55, a £4,500–£7,000 recon is 18–28% — ✅ safe. Against a £45,000 F87 CS, a £7,000–£10,000+ recon is 16–22% — ✅ safe. The M2's values are strong enough to justify engine work — but factor in preventative maintenance (crank hub fix £1,500–£3,000) as part of the purchase cost. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW M2 Engine Range Evolved — 2016 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW launched the M2 as the spiritual successor to the 1M Coupe. The N55 was chosen over the S55 to create a distinct character — more playful, less serious.
eras.worthKnowing: The N55B30T0 is a unique M2-specific engine — different pistons, rods, bearings, and cooling from the standard N55. It's robust but the crank hub is still a concern on modified cars.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The M2 Competition replaced the standard M2 with the S55 from the M3/M4 — 405 bhp, improved cooling, and a more serious character.
eras.worthKnowing: The S55 brought M3/M4 performance to the M2 — but also the crank hub weakness. The Competition is the sweet spot in the used market.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The M2 CS was the ultimate F87 — 444 bhp, carbon fibre, and just 2,200 examples produced.
eras.worthKnowing: A future classic. The CS is expensive but values are holding strong. The S55's crank hub is still the caveat.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The second-generation M2 arrived on the CLAR platform — bigger, more powerful, and with the S58 engine from the G80 M3.
eras.worthKnowing: The S58 is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern performance engine. The G87 is the last manual M car. Buy it while you can.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW M2?

profiles.verdict: The M2 is built for this — 405 bhp (Competition) to 480 bhp (G87), rear-wheel-drive, and available with a manual gearbox. The F87 Competition is the used sweet spot; the G87 is the future. Buy the newest S58 you can afford, or hunt for a well-maintained S55 Competition.

The F87 M2 CS is a future classic — 444 bhp, carbon fibre, and just 2,200 examples produced. The F87 Competition is the modern classic — 405 bhp, proven engine, and the last of the analogue M cars. Both are worth proper investment.

The M2 is a daily-drivable sports car — the ride is firm but acceptable, the boot is practical, and the rear seats are usable. The G87 is more comfortable than the F87; the F87 Competition is the better weekend car.

The M2 is one of the best track-day cars you can buy — compact, rear-wheel-drive, and with exceptional chassis balance. The F87 Competition is the track-day sweet spot; the G87 is faster but heavier. Budget for cooling upgrades and crank hub prevention.

A £25,000 high-mileage F87 N55 is the entry point — but budget for crank hub prevention (£1,500–£3,000) and regular maintenance. The F87 Competition is worth the extra money — 405 bhp and a more robust engine for £30,000–£35,000.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My BMW M2 Engine — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: Crank hub spin on an S55? Cold-start rattle on an N55? On the M2, the answer is almost always repair — the values are strong enough to justify engine work. Get a personalised repair-vs-replace verdict using real UK cost data.
